steps.title: Use the shape of the scatterplot
steps.content: The data rise and then fall, so the model should be a downward-opening parabola. That means the coefficient of $x^2$ must be negative; equations with a positive $x^2$ coefficient open upward instead.

steps.title: Use the intercept size
steps.content: The data values are in the hundreds, and the model should have a positive $y$-intercept around $745$. $y = -1.674x^2 -19.76x -745.73$ has a negative intercept, $-745.73$, so it is not reasonable.

steps.title: Confirm the model
steps.content: $y = -1.674x^2 + 19.76x + 745.73$, $$y=-1.674x^2+19.76x+745.73,$$ opens downward and has the correct positive intercept. Therefore D is correct.
